title: HPC Profiler Event Schema
description: Shared event schema for CPU and GPU profiler streams. Version 0.1 — Phase 0 contract.

EventType.description: The kind of profiling event. Shared across CPU and GPU events.

TransferKind.description: Direction of a GPU memory transfer.

BaseEvent.description: Fields present on every event regardless of source.

event_id.description: Monotonically increasing integer. Unique within a single profiling session.

timestamp_ns.description: Wall-clock time in nanoseconds since session start. Used for temporal correlation.

process_id.description: OS process ID of the profiled process.

thread_id.description: OS thread ID. Used to distinguish concurrent allocation streams.

CpuEvent.description: A memory event captured by the Python profiler. Emitted on allocation, deallocation, or GC collection.

alloc_address.description: Memory address of the Python object. PRIMARY correlation key — matched against GPU src_address and dst_address.

alloc_size_bytes.description: Size of the allocation in bytes. Used as secondary correlation key when address matching is ambiguous.

object_type.description: Python type name of the allocated object, e.g. 'numpy.ndarray', 'torch.Tensor', 'list'.

ref_count.description: Python reference count at the moment of this event.

callstack.description: Python call stack at time of allocation, outermost frame first. Captured via tracemalloc.

gc_generation.description: Python GC generation (0, 1, or 2). Indicates object age and collection frequency.

is_dealloc.description: True if this event represents a deallocation. Pairs with an earlier ALLOC event at the same alloc_address.

GpuEvent.description: A memory event captured by the GPU profiler via CUPTI. Emitted on memory transfer, page fault, or kernel execution.

device_id.description: CUDA device index. Supports multi-GPU workloads.

src_address.description: Source memory address of the transfer. Matched against CpuEvent.alloc_address for HOST_TO_DEVICE transfers.

dst_address.description: Destination memory address of the transfer. Matched against CpuEvent.alloc_address for DEVICE_TO_HOST transfers.

transfer_size_bytes.description: Size of the memory transfer in bytes. Secondary correlation key.

um_page_faults.description: Number of Unified Memory page faults associated with this event. 0 for explicit cudaMemcpy transfers.

kernel_name.description: CUDA kernel name if this event is a KERNEL type. Empty string for transfer and page fault events.

CorrelatedEvent.description: Output of the correlator. Links a CpuEvent to a GpuEvent with a confidence score.

cpu_event_id.description: References BaseEvent.event_id of the matched CpuEvent.

gpu_event_id.description: References BaseEvent.event_id of the matched GpuEvent.

confidence.description: HARD = address match confirmed. WEAK = timestamp + size match only.

match_reason.description: Which correlation keys produced this match.

latency_ns.description: Time between the CpuEvent and GpuEvent timestamps in nanoseconds. Represents CPU-to-GPU transfer latency.

ProfilingSession.description: Top-level container for a complete profiling run. This is the JSON file the web UI loads.

session_id.description: Unique identifier for this profiling session, e.g. a UUID.

schema_version.description: Schema version string, e.g. '0.1'. Allows the UI to handle future schema changes.

start_time_ns.description: Session start wall-clock time in nanoseconds.

end_time_ns.description: Session end wall-clock time in nanoseconds.

cpu_events.description: All CPU memory events captured during the session.

gpu_events.description: All GPU memory events captured during the session.

correlated_events.description: All matched CPU-GPU event pairs produced by the correlator.
